text: The intersection \(A \cap B\) contains elements that are _____.
The union \(A \cup B\) includes elements that are _____.
For sets \(A\) and \(B\), what does \(A \cup B\) represent?
The set \( \bigcap_{i=1}^n A_i \) denotes the intersection of _____.
d: only the first and last set

text: Which is true about \(A \cup (B \cap C)\) compared to \((A \cup B) \cap C\)?
The associative law for union states that \(A \cup B \cup C\) means:
answer: Order of union operations does not matter
a: Union evaluated left to right only
b: Union evaluated right to left only
c: Order of union operations does not matter
d: Only unions of two sets are allowed

text: In a Venn diagram, the shaded area for \(A \cap B\) is the region where:
The set \(A \cap B\) contains elements that are in _____.
If \(A\) is all multiples of 2, and \(B\) all multiples of 3, \(A \cap B\) is the set of multiples of _____.
The associative law for union means \(A \cup (B \cup C)\) equals _____.
The expression \(\bigcap_{i=1}^n A_i\) means the set of elements that are in _____.
The union \(\bigcup_{i=1}^n A_i\) contains elements that are in _____.
Consider sets \(A = \{1,2,4\}\), \(B = \{2,4,7\}\), and \(C = \{2,3,5\}\). The set \(A \cup (B \cap C)\) contains which element(s)?
The union \(A \cup B\) of finite sets is always _____.
Which statement best describes the difference between union and intersection?
answer: Union includes elements in either set; intersection includes elements common to both sets.
a: Union requires elements to be in both sets; intersection requires elements in one set.
b: Union includes elements in either set; intersection includes elements common to both sets.
c: Union and intersection always produce the same set.
d: Union excludes elements common to both sets; intersection includes all elements.
